children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award supplemental grants to States determined to be eligible under subsection (b) to enable such States to provide comprehensive services of the type described in to supplement the services otherwise provided by the State under a grant under this subpart in emerging communities within the State that are not eligible to receive grants under part A.section 300ff–22(a) of this title
be eligible to receive a grant under this subpart;
demonstrate the existence in the State of an emerging community as defined in subsection (d)(1);

agree that the grant will be used to provide funds directly to emerging communities in the State, separately from other funds under this subchapter that are provided by the State to such communities; and
submit the information described in subsection (c).
a report concerning the dissemination of supplemental funds under this section and the plan for the utilization of such funds in the emerging community;
a demonstration of the existing commitment of local resources, both financial and in-kind;

a demonstration that the State will maintain HIV-related activities at a level that is equal to not less than the level of such activities in the State for the 1-year period preceding the fiscal year for which the State is applying to receive a grant under ;section 300ff–21 of this title
a demonstration of the ability of the State to utilize such supplemental financial resources in a manner that is immediately responsive and cost effective;

a demonstration that the resources will be allocated in accordance with the local demographic incidence of AIDS including appropriate allocations for services for infants, children, women, and families with HIV/AIDS;
a demonstration of the inclusiveness of the planning process, with particular emphasis on affected communities and individuals with HIV/AIDS; and
a demonstration of the manner in which the proposed services are consistent with local needs assessments and the statewide coordinated statement of need.

children.heading: Definitions of emerging community
children.label: d Definitions of emerging community
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “emerging community” means a metropolitan area (as defined in ) for which there has been reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention a cumulative total of at least 500, but fewer than 1,000, cases of AIDS during the most recent period of 5 calendar years for which such data are available.section 300ff–17 of this title

children.heading: Continued status as emerging community
children.label: e Continued status as emerging community

children.text: to meet the requirements of subsection (d); and

to have a cumulative total of 750 or more living cases of AIDS (reported to and confirmed by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) as of December 31 of the most recent calendar year for which such data is available.
the amount available under for the fiscal year; andsection 300ff–31b(b)(1) of this title

a percentage equal to the ratio constituted by the number of living cases of HIV/AIDS in emerging communities in the State to the sum of the respective numbers of such cases in such communities for all States.
